sourceMovie.title: Splash
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Splash (1984) as a comedy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Just My Luck
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Splash clicked for you, Just My Luck (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Splash?
faq.answer: Try Just My Luck, Aquamarine, The Purple Rose of Cairo—each shares DNA with Splash while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Splash?
faq.answer: Splash (1984) is best described as Comedy, Romance, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Splash part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Splash Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
